title: Apeiris Evidence Claim
description: Schema for a single AI model assurance claim object. A claim links a control reference to a specific test result, observed evidence, regulatory obligation coverage, and a pass/fail decision. Claims are produced by the model operator, deployer, or independent auditor and consumed by the Apeiris verifier to construct an attestation graph.

schema.description: Claim schema URI. Always apeiris://model/claims/v1 for this schema version.

claim_id.description: Globally unique claim identifier in apeiris:// URI format. Encodes the control reference and a short slug describing the specific claim.

claim.description: Human-readable statement of what is being claimed. Must be falsifiable and specific enough to evaluate against the threshold.

control_ref.description: The control ID this claim tests, matching the ID in the AI Model Assurance Control Matrix.

control_name.description: Human-readable name of the control being tested.

layer.description: The assurance layer this control belongs to: LI (Lineage), TG (Training/Data), EV (Evaluation), OA (Governance), BH (Deployment/Runtime), CR (Continuous Risk).

test_method.description: How the test was conducted. Use canonical method names where possible.

test_method_version.description: Version or release of the test methodology or tool suite used. Include if the methodology is versioned.

threshold.description: The pass criterion. State the condition that must be true for the decision to be 'pass' or 'conditional-pass'.

observed_result.description: What the test actually measured. Must be comparable to the threshold to justify the decision.

confidence.description: Evaluator confidence in the observed result and decision. High: repeatable, statistically validated, independently verified. Medium: tested but limited sample or methodology. Low: limited evidence, extrapolated, or estimated.

evidence_artifacts.description: One or more evidence artifacts that support this claim. Each artifact must be independently retrievable and verifiable.

artifact_id.description: Unique identifier for this artifact within the claim.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this artifact is and what it proves.

location.description: URI, S3 path, or other stable locator for the artifact. Use internal URIs if the artifact is not publicly accessible.

hash.description: Content hash of the artifact for integrity verification.

collected_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time when this artifact was collected or produced.

obligation_links.description: Regulatory or standards obligations this claim helps satisfy. Links the evidence directly to compliance obligations, enabling automated gap analysis.

obligation_instrument.description: The regulatory instrument or standard. Use the framework keys from the AI Model Assurance Control Matrix where applicable.

provision.description: Specific provision, article, section, or clause.

actor_role.description: The actor role to which this obligation attaches for this claim.

assurance_target.description: Identifies who and what this claim is about — the model, its deployment context, and use case.

model_provider.description: Organization that trained or provides the model.

model_id.description: Model identifier, version, or checkpoint hash. Must be stable and unambiguous.

deployment_context.description: Where and how the model is deployed.

use_case.description: The intended use case for which this claim applies.

tested_by.description: Who performed the test. 'internal' means the deploying organization ran the test itself. 'independent-auditor' means a third party with no stake in the outcome. 'automated-pipeline' means a fully automated CI/CD or monitoring system.

tested_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time when the test was performed or the observation period ended.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time when this claim must be re-validated. After this date the claim should be treated as expired and not relied on for compliance.

decision.description: The outcome of evaluating the observed result against the threshold. conditional-pass: passes with documented exceptions or compensating controls.

decision_rationale.description: Explanation of why this decision was reached. Must reference the threshold and the observed result. For conditional-pass, must describe the conditions and compensating controls.

claim_version.description: Version of this claim record. Increment when the claim is re-evaluated or updated.

produced_by.description: Tool, system, or process that produced this claim record.
